title: Pseudoponera tenuissima
taxonomy: Pseudoponera tenuissima belongs to the family Formicidae, commonly known as ants. This species is classified under the subfamily Ponerinae, which includes several species of ants known as "ponerines." Within the Ponerinae subfamily, Pseudoponera tenuissima is further classified into the tribe Ponerini. The tribe Ponerini is a group of ants characterized by their elongated bodies and small size. Pseudoponera tenuissima is a species of ant that is native to various regions, including the Americas.
constituents: Pseudoponera tenuissima is an ant species known for its unique characteristics, but information regarding its active constituents is limited. Research on this species is mostly focused on its behavior and habitat. However, in general, ant species like Pseudoponera tenuissima often contain active constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenoids. These compounds can have various biological activities, including antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. More studies are needed to determine the specific active constituents of Pseudoponera tenuissima and their potential uses.
